title: Control Board Internal Failure
shortDescription: The electronic oven control board has failed an internal self-diagnostic test.
fullDescription: Error F1 on Jenn-Air ovens indicates the electronic oven control (EOC) has detected a hardware failure in its processor, memory, or relay circuits. As Jenn-Air is manufactured by Whirlpool, this code follows the Whirlpool F-code system. The oven will not operate until the board is replaced.

causes.cause: Control board processor or EEPROM failure

fixSteps.title: Power cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the circuit breaker for 5 minutes. If F1 was a temporary glitch, it may clear.

fixSteps.title: Check board connections
fixSteps.instruction: Access the control board and reseat all connectors. Look for heat damage or corrosion.

fixSteps.title: Inspect for visible damage
fixSteps.instruction: Look for burned components or swollen capacitors.

fixSteps.title: Replace the control board
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Document wire positions. Swap the board and reconnect.

videoSearchQuery: Jenn-Air oven F1 control board error fix

faq.q: Are Jenn-Air parts the same as Whirlpool?
faq.a: Jenn-Air is owned by Whirlpool Corporation. Many control boards and parts are shared or cross-referenced between the brands.

faq.q: Can F1 be fixed without replacing the board?
faq.a: Rarely. Reseating connectors occasionally helps, but most F1 codes require board replacement.

faq.q: Where can I find Jenn-Air parts?
faq.a: Jenn-Air parts are available from authorized dealers, Whirlpool Parts, and online appliance parts retailers.

title: Oven Over-Temperature
shortDescription: The oven cavity has exceeded the maximum safe temperature.
fullDescription: Error F2 on Jenn-Air ovens indicates the oven has exceeded its safe temperature limit. The control shuts down heating elements. This is a critical safety code caused by a faulty sensor, stuck relay, or shorted element.

causes.cause: Bake or broil relay stuck closed

fixSteps.title: Cool the oven
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off and open door carefully. Wait 30-60 minutes.

fixSteps.title: Test temperature sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Measure resistance at rear connector. Should be 1080-1100 ohms at room temperature.

fixSteps.title: Replace sensor if faulty
fixSteps.instruction: One mounting screw inside oven. Pull out and install replacement.

fixSteps.title: Check for stuck relay
fixSteps.instruction: If sensor is good, the board relay is stuck. Board replacement required.

whenToCallPro: If sensor is good, stuck relay is a fire hazard. Disconnect power and call a pro.
videoSearchQuery: Jenn-Air oven F2 over temperature error fix

faq.a: Yes. An overheating oven is a fire hazard. Disconnect power until repaired.
Yes. Self-clean temperatures can trigger F2 if the sensor has drifted.

faq.q: What is the temperature sensor part number?
faq.a: Most Jenn-Air ovens use Whirlpool part WPW10181986 or equivalent.

title: Temperature Sensor Open Circuit
shortDescription: The oven temperature sensor circuit reads as open.
fullDescription: Error F3 indicates the temperature sensor has an open circuit. The oven cannot read temperature and will not heat.

fixSteps.title: Check connections
fixSteps.instruction: Access rear panel. Ensure sensor connector is plugged in.

fixSteps.title: Test resistance
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect sensor. Should read 1080-1100 ohms at room temp. OL means open.

fixSteps.title: Check wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Trace wires for breaks near the oven cavity.

fixSteps.title: Replace sensor
fixSteps.instruction: One screw inside oven. Pull out old, install new.

whenToCallPro: If a new sensor does not clear F3, the board needs diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Jenn-Air oven F3 temperature sensor error fix

faq.q: Is the sensor easy to replace?
faq.a: Yes. One of the simplest oven repairs.

faq.q: Can I use the oven with F3?
faq.a: No. Without a sensor, temperature cannot be regulated.

faq.q: What is the difference between F2 and F3?
faq.a: F2 is actual overheating. F3 is a broken sensor circuit.

title: Temperature Sensor Shorted
shortDescription: The oven temperature sensor has a short circuit.
fullDescription: Error F4 indicates the oven temperature sensor has a short circuit, reading near zero resistance. The control interprets this as extreme heat and shuts down.

fixSteps.title: Test sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Should read 1080-1100 ohms. Near zero indicates short.

fixSteps.title: Check for moisture
fixSteps.instruction: Dry the connector with compressed air.

fixSteps.title: Check wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Look for pinched or bare wires touching.

fixSteps.title: Replace sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove mounting screw and install replacement.

whenToCallPro: If new sensor does not fix F4.
videoSearchQuery: Jenn-Air oven F4 sensor shorted error fix

faq.a: Yes. Condensation in the connector can create a temporary short.

faq.q: Is F4 the same fix as F3?
faq.a: Same sensor, same replacement procedure. F3 is open, F4 is shorted.

faq.q: Does Jenn-Air use the same sensor as Whirlpool?
faq.a: Yes, many models share the same Whirlpool-manufactured sensor.

title: Door Latch Error
shortDescription: The door latch mechanism has failed to lock or unlock properly.
fullDescription: Error F5 indicates the door lock motor or latch switch is not functioning. Self-clean cannot proceed without a confirmed door lock.

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off breaker for 5 minutes to free a stuck door.

fixSteps.title: Clean the latch
fixSteps.instruction: Remove debris from the latch at the top of the door opening.

fixSteps.title: Test latch switches
fixSteps.instruction: Test continuity in locked and unlocked positions.

fixSteps.title: Replace latch assembly
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Access and replace the latch.

whenToCallPro: If the door is stuck locked after a power reset.
videoSearchQuery: Jenn-Air oven F5 door latch error fix

faq.a: Usually yes. F5 only affects self-clean.

faq.q: Is the latch the same as Whirlpool?
faq.a: Many Jenn-Air latch assemblies are cross-compatible with Whirlpool models.

faq.q: How do I unlock a stuck door?
faq.a: Power reset usually works. If not, look for a manual release.

title: Relay Board Failure
shortDescription: A heating element relay has failed on the control board.
fullDescription: Error F6 indicates a bake or broil relay has failed. It may be stuck open or closed, preventing proper heating control.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off breaker for 5 minutes.

fixSteps.title: Test elements
fixSteps.instruction: Check bake (20-50 ohms) and broil (15-40 ohms) resistance.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the board
fixSteps.instruction: Look for burned relay area.

fixSteps.title: Replace the board
fixSteps.instruction: Swap control board, reconnect all wiring.

whenToCallPro: If oven heats continuously, disconnect power and call a professional immediately.
videoSearchQuery: Jenn-Air oven F6 relay board failure fix

faq.a: Yes. A stuck closed relay means continuous heating. Disconnect power.
Yes. A shorted element can damage a new board.
Some repair services do board-level relay repair at lower cost.

title: Stuck Touchpad Key
shortDescription: A touchpad key is stuck in a pressed state.
fullDescription: Error F7 indicates a specific key on the touchpad is registering as continuously pressed, preventing normal operation.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off breaker for 5 minutes.

fixSteps.title: Clean touchpad
fixSteps.instruction: Clean with damp cloth. Dry thoroughly.

fixSteps.title: Disconnect touchpad
fixSteps.instruction: If F7 clears with touchpad disconnected, it is the problem.

fixSteps.title: Replace touchpad
fixSteps.instruction: Install new touchpad membrane.

whenToCallPro: If replacing the touchpad does not fix F7.
videoSearchQuery: Jenn-Air oven F7 stuck key touchpad error fix

faq.a: Only by turning off the circuit breaker.
Yes. Order the exact part for your Jenn-Air model.
Yes. Humidity or steam can cause false touches on the touchpad membrane.

title: Configuration Error
shortDescription: The control board EEPROM configuration data is corrupted.
fullDescription: Error F8 indicates the EEPROM on the control board has corrupted data. The board cannot operate without valid calibration and model-specific configuration.

fixSteps.title: Extended power cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off breaker for 10 minutes.

fixSteps.title: Verify board part number
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure correct board is installed for your model.

fixSteps.title: Check connections
fixSteps.instruction: Reseat all board connectors.

fixSteps.title: Replace the board
fixSteps.instruction: F8 requires board replacement.

whenToCallPro: F8 almost always requires board replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Jenn-Air oven F8 EEPROM configuration error fix

faq.a: Not in the field. Board replacement is required.
Power surges or outages during write operations.
F8 is uncommon but well-documented.

title: Door Lock Timeout
shortDescription: The door lock motor ran but did not complete its cycle.
fullDescription: Error F9 indicates the door lock motor could not achieve the expected state within the time limit. Self-clean cannot operate.

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off breaker for 5 minutes.

fixSteps.title: Clean latch
fixSteps.instruction: Remove debris from latch mechanism.

fixSteps.title: Test lock switch
fixSteps.instruction: Test continuity in both positions.

fixSteps.title: Replace lock assembly
fixSteps.instruction: Install new lock assembly.

whenToCallPro: If door is stuck locked after power reset.
videoSearchQuery: Jenn-Air oven F9 door lock timeout error fix

faq.q: Is F9 the same as F5?
faq.a: Similar. F5 is a switch state error. F9 is a timeout.
Usually yes, F9 only affects self-clean.
No more than once per month to avoid stressing components.

title: Runaway Temperature
shortDescription: The oven temperature is rising uncontrollably.
fullDescription: Error F10 is a critical safety code indicating the oven is heating beyond the set temperature and the control cannot stop it. A stuck relay allows continuous power to an element.

causes.cause: Relay welded closed on the board

fixSteps.title: Disconnect power immediately
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off breaker now. This is a fire hazard.

fixSteps.title: Cool the oven
fixSteps.instruction: Wait 1 hour for complete cooling.

fixSteps.title: Test sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If sensor is fine, the relay is stuck.

fixSteps.title: Replace the board
fixSteps.instruction: Board replacement required for stuck relay. Also check elements.

whenToCallPro: F10 is a fire hazard. Call immediately.
videoSearchQuery: Jenn-Air oven F10 runaway temperature error fix

faq.a: Yes. Disconnect power immediately.

faq.q: How is F10 different from F2?
faq.a: F2 detects over-temp. F10 means the control cannot stop the heating — more severe.
Some services repair boards at lower cost than replacement.

title: Communication Error
shortDescription: Communication failure between the control board and interface board.
fullDescription: Error F11 indicates the main control board and user interface board cannot communicate over their serial data link.
symptoms: Oven may run on last setting

fixSteps.title: Power cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off breaker for 5 minutes.

fixSteps.title: Reseat cable
fixSteps.instruction: Firmly reseat the connecting cable on both boards.

fixSteps.title: Inspect for damage
fixSteps.instruction: Check cable and connectors for heat or corrosion damage.

fixSteps.title: Replace faulty board
fixSteps.instruction: Technician determines which board failed.

whenToCallPro: If reseating cables does not fix F11.
videoSearchQuery: Jenn-Air oven F11 communication error fix

faq.a: Yes. Loose connections cause intermittent errors.

faq.q: Is it safe to use with intermittent F11?
faq.a: No. Loss of communication during operation is unsafe.
Both fail at similar rates. Professional testing identifies which.

title: Convection Fan Motor Fault
shortDescription: The convection fan motor is not operating at expected speed.
fullDescription: Error F12 indicates the convection fan has stalled, is too slow, or did not start. Convection cooking cannot function without the fan.

fixSteps.title: Check for obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Remove racks and look at fan behind rear panel for debris.

fixSteps.title: Test motor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect and test resistance. Spin blade by hand.

fixSteps.title: Check connections
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure motor connector is firm. Look for heat-damaged wires.

fixSteps.title: Replace motor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove rear oven panel. Unbolt motor and install replacement.

whenToCallPro: If motor tests fine but F12 persists.
videoSearchQuery: Jenn-Air oven F12 convection fan motor error fix

faq.q: Can I use bake mode without convection?
faq.a: Some models allow standard bake with F12 active.

faq.q: How long do fan motors last?
faq.a: 8-15 years under normal use.
$50-$130 for the part, typical for premium brands.
